meaning: n.(高山等上之)常积不消的冰,冰盖
examples: Some 95 percent of Antarctica is covered by an icecap averaging .
Large flat sheets of the icecap called ice shelves float in water near Antarctica.
Some 95 percent of Antarctica is covered by an icecap averaging 1.6 km (1 mi) in thickness.
Measuring the thickness of the ice will help scientists discover how quickly the icecap is melting.
Mr. THOMAS DEUTSCH (Deputy Executive Director, American Securitization Forum): The securitization markets are as frozen as a polar icecap.
